@@ -356,7 +356,7 @@
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.27","title":"Computed range expansion (for-each over expressions)","description":"Support for-each expansion over computed ranges, not just static lists:

for-each:
  var: move_num
  range: "1..2^{disks}"  # Computed at cook time

This enables formulas like Towers of Hanoi where the step count is a function of input variables. The range expression is evaluated during cooking.

Related: towers-of-hanoi.formula.yaml demonstrates the need.
Depends on: gt-8tmz.8 (Runtime dynamic expansion)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:27:02.71296-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T19:27:02.71296-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.27","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:27:02.713399-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.27","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T19:27:08.191351-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.28","title":"Formula error handling and recovery","description":"Specify how cooking errors are reported and recovered:
- What happens when extends chain is broken (missing formula)?
- What happens when aspect pointcut matches nothing?
- What happens when expansion produces invalid structure?
- Graceful degradation vs fail-fast semantics
- Error metadata in cooked protos

Deferred: post-launch polish","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:06:28.452872-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:06:28.452872-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.28","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:06:28.453346-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.29","title":"Formula versioning and migration strategy","description":"Define how formula versions are managed:
- What does the version field mean? Semver? Integer?
- When a formula changes, what happens to existing protos?
- Do protos track their source formula version?
- Migration path: old proto + new formula = ?
- Backwards compatibility guarantees (if any)

Deferred: post-launch polish","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:06:45.764277-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:06:45.764277-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.29","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:06:45.764686-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-8tmz.3","title":"Expansion operators: expand and map (macros)","description":"Implement macro-style expansion operators:
- expand(target, template) - apply template to single step
- map(molecule, template) - apply template to all matching steps

Templates use {target} substitution for step references.
Rule of Five is the canonical example.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:31.472199-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:28:37.052195-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:31.472662-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:49.809887-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:48:18.717567-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-8tmz.3","title":"Expansion operators: expand and map (macros)","description":"Implement macro-style expansion operators:
- expand(target, template) - apply template to single step
- map(molecule, template) - apply template to all matching steps

Templates use {target} substitution for step references.
Rule of Five is the canonical example.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:31.472199-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T11:33:56.821674-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:31.472662-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:49.809887-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.3","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:48:18.717567-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.30","title":"Proto debugging and inspection tools","description":"Tools for understanding cooked protos:
- bd cook --dry-run (already planned)
- Step-through cooking: see each compose rule applied
- Diff between proto versions
- Provenance tracing: which formula/rule produced this step?
- Visualization of step graph (ASCII or graphviz)

 {"id":"gt-8tmz.31","title":"Formula validation specification","description":"Complete validation rules for formulas:
- Required vs optional fields
- Valid step ID patterns
- Valid pointcut glob syntax
- Variable reference validation
- Compose rule ordering constraints
- Type-specific validation (workflow vs expansion vs aspect)

 {"id":"gt-8tmz.32","title":"Consolidate molecular-chemistry.md with rig-cook-run.md","description":"Merge rig-cook-run.md into molecular-chemistry.md as the canonical chemical algebra spec:

- Rig operator at source level
- Unified vocabulary
